Pandanus julianettinus is a species of plant that belongs to the Pandanus genus, a group of tropical plants commonly known as screwpine or pandan. The leaves of Pandanus julianettinus have been used in traditional medicine for their health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as the ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pandanus julianettinus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
